title: Seebeck Effect and Thermoelectric Power Generation
content: When a temperature gradient ΔT exists across a thermoelectric junction, an open-circuit voltage V_oc = S·ΔT is generated, where S is the Seebeck coefficient (μV/K). Maximum power transfer occurs when load resistance equals internal resistance: P_max = S²·ΔT²/(4·R_int). Chemical processes with persistent thermal gradients (e.g., exothermic reactors, distillation columns) are candidate sources for thermoelectric energy harvesting.
